target_fund_identity.format_clue: Narrative paragraph on page 5, column header in financial statements on page 14, and a row in a table of sub-funds on page 22.
target_fund_identity.evidence_quote:
Page 5: Trojan Fund (Ireland) Market Review The Trojan Fund (Ireland) (the “Sub-Fund”) Class O GBP accumulation shares returned +0.5% in the financial period. 
Page 22: Trojan Fund (Ireland) ... Launch date 13 February 2012

fund_size_history.format_clue: A row item 'Net assets attributable to holders of redeemable participating shares' in a columnar table (Statement of financial position and Statement of changes in net assets) with one column per sub-fund.
fund_size_history.evidence_quote:
Page 14 (as at 31 July 2025): Net assets attributable to holders of redeemable participating shares 562,965,276
Page 15 (as at 31 January 2025): Net assets attributable to holders of redeemable participating shares 563,639,155

share_classes_catalog.format_clue: A table in Note 11 provides the number of shares in circulation (opening, issued, redeemed, closing) for each class. A separate table in Note 14 provides the NAV per share for each class at two dates.
share_classes_catalog.evidence_quote:
Page 32: Trojan Fund (Ireland) Class I EUR accumulation 51,321,618.74 11,260,497.75 (7,747,734.89) 54,834,381.60
Page 40: Trojan Fund (Ireland) Net asset value per share Currency 31 July 2025 31 January 2025 Class I EUR accumulation EUR 1.2091 1.2238

fee_structure.format_clue: Narrative text and a table of rates per share class in Note 6. Line items in the Statement of Comprehensive Income on page 16. A table of Total Expense Ratios (TER) for Swiss investors on page 67.
fee_structure.evidence_quote:
Page 28: Trojan Fund (Ireland) ... All Class O shares 1.00% ... All Class X shares 0.85%
Page 67: Total Expense Ratio Trojan Fund (Ireland) ... Class O GBP accumulation 1.11%

fund_economics_yearly.format_clue: Columnar table (Statement of comprehensive income) showing income, expenses, and the resulting increase/decrease in net assets for the period. The target fund has its own column.
fund_economics_yearly.evidence_quote: Page 16: Trojan Fund (Ireland) ... Annual management charge 2,736,281 ... Total operating expenses 2,958,539 ... Increase/(decrease) in net assets attributable to holders of redeemable participating shares from continuing operations (4,983,011)

currency_conversion_rates.format_clue: A table in Note 9 listing spot foreign exchange rates used to convert assets and liabilities to the functional currency (GBP and USD) at the reporting date.
currency_conversion_rates.evidence_quote: Page 30: 9. Exchange rates. The following spot foreign exchange rates were used to convert the assets and liabilities held in foreign currencies other than the functional currency of the Company at the reporting date. 31 July 2025 ... Currency Exchange rate to GBP ... Euro 1.156283

asset_allocation_history.format_clue: The Schedule of Investments provides a detailed breakdown of holdings grouped by asset type (Equities, Debt securities, etc.) with subtotals for fair value and % of NAV for one date (31 July 2025).
asset_allocation_history.evidence_quote: Page 48: Trojan Fund (Ireland) ... Total equities (2025: 30.88%) 219,141,918 38.93% ... Total debt securities (2025: 67.85%) 342,138,324 60.77%

geographic_allocation_history.format_clue: The Schedule of Investments groups holdings by country of issuer (e.g., Canada, France, United States) for one date (31 July 2025).
geographic_allocation_history.evidence_quote: Page 48: Trojan Fund (Ireland) ... Equities Canada ... France ... Ireland ... Netherlands ... Switzerland ... United Kingdom ... United States

portfolio_metrics.format_clue: Benchmark is stated in narrative text on pages 5 and 22. A specific drawdown figure is in a footnote on page 5. Weighted average duration is mentioned in narrative on page 6. Number of positions and top 10 concentration can be derived from the Schedule of Investments on pages 48-49.
portfolio_metrics.evidence_quote:
Page 5: For comparative purposes, the UK Retail Price Index (the “Index”) returned +3.3% ... The Sub-Fund fell -2.7%, peak-to-trough, versus -12.8% for the FTSE All-Share Index (TR).
Page 6: ...we reduced the Sub-Fund’s weighted average duration to these bonds from five years to two years...

top_holdings.format_clue: A detailed list of all portfolio holdings in the 'Schedule of investments', including name, currency, nominal holdings, fair value, and % of NAV.
top_holdings.evidence_quote: Page 48: Trojan Fund (Ireland) ... Equities ... United Kingdom Diageo PLC GBP 950,675 17,616,008 3.13% Unilever PLC GBP 610,429 26,907,710 4.78%

performance_history.format_clue: A single sentence in the narrative 'Investment Manager's report' stating the return for one share class for the six-month financial period, compared to its benchmark.
performance_history.evidence_quote: The Trojan Fund (Ireland) (the “Sub-Fund”) Class O GBP accumulation shares returned +0.5% in the financial period. For comparative purposes, the UK Retail Price Index (the “Index”) returned +3.3% and the Bank of England Base Rate returned +2.2% over the same period.

manager_qualitative.format_clue: Narrative paragraphs in the 'Investment Manager's report' (Portfolio Review, Outlook) describing the investment approach and positioning. The official objective is stated in a table in Note 1.
manager_qualitative.evidence_quote:
Page 5: The Sub-Fund is positioned to withstand a broad range of different environments, and our approach is always to remain patient and lean into risk when we are rewarded to do so.
Page 6: Against this backdrop, we remain conservatively positioned, with a relatively low weighting to equities...

manager_thesis_and_decisions.format_clue: Narrative paragraphs in the 'Investment Manager's report' detailing the market view (thesis), specific portfolio changes (decisions), and rationale for those changes during the period.
manager_thesis_and_decisions.evidence_quote:
Page 5: Within the equity component of the portfolio, we added to existing holdings in Agilent Technologies, Alphabet, Chubb and Diageo and established new holdings in Adobe, L’Oréal, Hubbell, Canadian National Railway, LSEG and LVMH.
Page 6: We exited Procter & Gamble (P&G) and Moody’s...
